mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months following a rate cut, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces equity valuations lower over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment surge.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust economic demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into immediate positive S&P 500 price momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >65% of months following industrial production acceleration, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 confirm robust labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200k in a single month
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are negative in >55% of months following payrolls above the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in advanced retail sales indicates expanding consumer spending power and direct revenue tailwinds for S&P 500 companies, producing positive forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months following retail sales growth, or the correlation between retail sales momentum and equity returns falls below 0.10 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and housing starts signal broad-based supply-side recovery and construction sector strength, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal generates negative forward returns in >50% of months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: A discrete increase in the federal funds rate raises the risk-free discount rate and compresses equity multiples, causing immediate negative S&P 500 price adjustments over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >= 0.25% in a single meeting
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of rate hike events, or the post-hike return distribution does not significantly differ from zero (t-test p > 0.05).
